<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 8 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- llms.txt and context.md are AI consumption artifacts regenerated by sourcevision; they should not be hand-edited
- tsconfig.json and vitest.config.ts are shared build/test roots; any changes here affect all downstream packages
- README.md and image assets (Rex.png, Rex-F.png) serve as onboarding artifacts and should stay synchronized with actual CLI behavior documented in CLAUDE.md
- Cohesion and coupling are both 0.0 — correct for config/doc files that have no import relationships.
- The presence of llms.txt and context.md here suggests sourcevision was run at the package root; confirm these are gitignored or intentionally committed as living documentation.
- vitest.config.ts at the root acts as the test runner anchor; ensure all package-level test configs properly extend or reference this root to avoid silent test isolation gaps.
- llms.txt and context.md appearing in the package-config zone alongside package.json and tsconfig.json indicates sourcevision was run with the rex package root as the target directory — these AI-consumption artifacts are co-mingled with build configuration in the same zone, suggesting no explicit gitignore or generation-time separation policy was applied at the package level.
- AI-generated artifacts (llms.txt, context.md) are co-located with build configuration files (tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts) in the same zone with no separation. If hand-edited or stale, they will mislead AI tooling without any regeneration guard or staleness detection.
- Rex.png and Rex-F.png are binary image assets with no programmatic consumers — they can never produce import edges and will always appear as isolated nodes in any import-graph-based zone analysis. Their zone assignment is an artifact of the Louvain algorithm having no other community to place them in, not a reflection of architectural intent. Binary assets should be excluded from import-graph zone analysis entirely.
- The zone name package-config implies configuration files, but the zone contains README.md, binary images (Rex.png, Rex-F.png), and AI-consumption artifacts (llms.txt, context.md) alongside actual configuration files (package.json, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts). The name masks the zone contents and would mislead a developer trying to locate documentation vs configuration.
- Binary assets (Rex.png, Rex-F.png) should be excluded from import-graph zone analysis — they are always isolated nodes and inflate the zone file count without contributing to import topology. Add them to a sourcevision exclusion list.
- Zone name package-config is misleading — the zone contains docs, images, and AI artifacts in addition to config files. Rename to package-root or docs-and-config to reflect actual contents.

</insights>
